           font-size: 12px;\n            color: #cbd5e1;\n            word-break: break-all;\n        }\n    </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n    <div class=\"container\">\n        <header>\n            <div style=\"display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:8px\">\n                <h1>ADC Canvas</h1>\n                <span id=\"versionBadge\" style=\"font-size:12px;color:#475569;font-weight:500;font-family:'Monaco','Menlo',monospace\"></span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"status\">\n                <span class=\"status-dot\" id=\"statusDot\"></span>\n                <span id=\"statusText\">Connecting...</span>\n            </div>\n        </header>\n\n        <div class=\"workload-summary\" id=\"workloadSummary\"></div>\n\n        <div class=\"input-bar\">\n            <textarea\n                id=\"inputTextarea\"\n                class=\"input-textarea\"\n                placeholder=\"Ask anything — status of the options pipeline, what's in flight, open beads…\"\n                rows=\"1\"\n            ></textarea>\n            <button class=\"btn-mic\" id=\"btnMic\" title=\"Voice input\">\n                <svg width=\"20\" height=\"20\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M12 2a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v7a3 3 0 0 0 6 0V5a3 3 0 0 0-3-3z\"/>\n                    <path d=\"M19 10v2a7 7 0 0 1-14 0v-2\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n            <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"btnSend\" title=\"Send\" disabled>\n                <svg width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <line x1=\"22\" y1=\"2\" x2=\"11\" y2=\"13\"/>\n                    <polygon points=\"22 2 15 22 11 13 2 9 22 2\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n        </div>\n\n        <div id=\"topicsContainer\" class=\"topics-grid\">\n            <div class=\"loading\">\n                <div class=\"spinner\"></div>\n                <span>Loading topics...</span>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n    <script>\n        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);\n        const sessionId = urlParams.get('session_id') || generateId();\n        const surfaceType = 'canvas';\n\n        let surfaceId = null;\n        let eventSource = null;\n\n        // Track pending placeholders so we can find/replace them later.\n        // Key: pendingId (utterance_id), Value: placeholder HTMLElement.\n        var pendingPlaceholders = {};\n\n        // Update URL with session_id for reload persistence\n        if (!urlParams.get('session_id')) {\n            urlParams.set('session_id', sessionId);\n            window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n        }\n\n        function generateId() {\n            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n        }\n\n        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n\n        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n\n            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n            statusText.textContent = text;\n        }\n\n        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n            const parts = [];\n\n            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n                el.classList.add('show');\n            } else {\n                el.classList.remove('show');\n            }\n        }\n\n        async function registerSurface() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n                return surfaceId;\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n                throw error;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n                const utterance = data.utterance;\n\n                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n\n                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                const createdAt = placeholder\n                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n                    : Date.now();\n\n                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                } else {\n                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n                }\n\n                // No longer track this placeholder\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n            });\n\n            // Thread-level progress event\n            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n\n                if (!threadId) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n\n                if (!intentId) return;\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n        // and apply aged treatment if they've exceeded the 30s threshold.\n        setInterval(function () {\n            var now = Date.now();\n            var pendingCards = document.querySelectorAll('.pending-card[data-pending-id]');\n            pendingCards.forEach(function (card) {\n                tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n                applyAgedTreatment(card, now);\n            });\n        }, 1000);\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. We'll swap it\n            // for the server's utterance_id when the response arrives.\n            var pendingId = 'pending-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 8);\n            var createdAt = Date.now();\n\n            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n            // so a hung server still leaves a card on canvas to age.\n            var placeholder = createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n            var container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n            // Clear the empty-state/loading spinner if present\n            if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n            }\n\n            // Prepend the placeholder (newest first)\n            container.insertBefore(placeholder, container.firstChild);\n\n            // Track it so we can find/replace it later\n            pendingPlaceholders[pendingId] = placeholder;\n\n            try {\n                var resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId, pending_id: pendingId }),\n                });\n\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    var errText = await resp.text();\n                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n                    // Try to parse as JSON for structured error\n                    var errData;\n                    try { errData = JSON.parse(errText); } catch (e) {}\n                    if (errData && errData.error_type) {\n                        // Replace placeholder with error card\n                        var errorCard = createErrorCard(errData);\n                        if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                            placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(errorCard, placeholder);\n                        }\n                        delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n                    }\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                var data = await resp.json();\n                // Swap to the server's utterance_id for consistency\n                var utteranceId = data.utterance_id || pendingId;\n                placeholder.dataset.pendingId = utteranceId;\n\n                // Move the placeholder tracking to the new key\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n                pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId] = placeholder;\n\n                // If intent_ids are present, split into per-thread pending cards\n                if (data.intent_ids && Array.isArray(data.intent_ids) && data.intent_ids.length > 0) {\n                    var threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, data.intent_ids);\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                        var frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                        threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                        placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                    }\n                    delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Dispatch failed:', err);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Web Speech API mic\n        let recognition = null;\n\n        if ('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window || 'SpeechRecognition' in window) {\n            const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n            recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\n            recognition.continuous = false;\n            recognition.interimResults = true;\n            recognition.lang = 'en-US';\n\n            let finalTranscript = '';\n\n            recognition.onstart = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                finalTranscript = textarea.value;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onresult = (e) => {\n                let interim = '';\n                for (let i = e.resultIndex; i < e.results.length; i++) {\n                    if (e.results[i].isFinal) {\n                        finalTranscript += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    } else {\n                        interim += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    }\n                }\n                textarea.value = finalTranscript + interim;\n                textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onend = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                if (finalTranscript.trim()) dispatch(finalTranscript.trim());\n            };\n\n            recognition.onerror = (e) => {\n                console.error('Speech recognition error:', e.error);\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n            };\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    recognition.stop();\n                } else {\n                    recognition.start();\n                }\n            });\n        } else {\n            // Fallback: MediaRecorder + whisper-stt for browsers without Web Speech API\n            let mediaRecorder = null;\n            let audioChunks = [];\n\n            async function startRecording() {\n                try {\n                    const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });\n                    mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(stream);\n                    audioChunks = [];\n\n                    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {\n                        if (event.data.size > 0) {\n                            audioChunks.push(event.data);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {\n                        btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                        btnMic.innerHTML = '🎤';\n\n                        // Create audio blob and convert to base64\n                        const audioBlob = new Blob(audioChunks, { type: 'audio/webm' });\n                        const reader = new FileReader();\n                        reader.onloadend = async () => {\n                            const base64Audio = reader.result.split(',')[1];\n\n                            try {\n                                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/stt', {\n                                    method: 'POST',\n                                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                                        audio_data: base64Audio,\n                                        format: 'webm'\n                                    })\n                                });\n\n                                if (resp.ok) {\n                                    const data = await resp.json();\n                                    if (data.text) {\n                                        textarea.value = data.text;\n                                        textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                                        textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                                        btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n                                        // Auto-dispatch on successful transcription\n                                        dispatch(data.text.trim());\n                                    }\n                                } else {\n                                    const err = await resp.json();\n                                    console.error('STT error:', err);\n                                    btnMic.title = `Transcription failed: ${err.error || 'Unknown error'}`;\n                                    setTimeout(() => {\n                                        btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                    }, 3000);\n                                }\n                            } catch (err) {\n                                console.error('STT request failed:', err);\n                                btnMic.title = 'STT service unavailable';\n                                setTimeout(() => {\n                                    btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                }, 3000);\n                            }\n                        };\n                        reader.readAsDataURL(audioBlob);\n\n                        // Stop all tracks to release microphone\n                        stream.getTracks().forEach(track => track.stop());\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.start();\n                    btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                    btnMic.innerHTML = '⏹';\n                } catch (err) {\n                    console.error('MediaRecorder access failed:', err);\n                    btnMic.title = 'Microphone access denied';\n                    btnMic.style.opacity = '0.4';\n                    btnMic.disabled = true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            function stopRecording() {\n                if (mediaRecorder && mediaRecorder.state === 'recording') {\n                    mediaRecorder.stop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n            btnMic.disabled = false;\n            btnMic.style.opacity = '1';\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    stopRecording();\n                } else {\n                    startRecording();\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        // ── Initialize ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        // Initialize\n        async function init() {\n            try {\n                // Show version\n                const meta = await fetch('/openapi.json').then(r => r.json()).catch(() => null);\n                if (meta && meta.info && meta.info.version) {\n                    document.getElementById('versionBadge').textContent = 'v' + meta.info.version;\n                }\n\n                await registerSurface();\n                connectSSE();\n            } catch (error) {\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Failed to connect');\n                console.error('Initialization error:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        init();\n    </script>\n\n    <!-- ── Agentation: element-selector feedback toolbar ────────────────────── -->\n    <script>\n    (function () {\n        var active = false;\n        var selectedEl = null;\n\n        // Build a stable CSS selector for a given element\n        function buildSelector(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) return 'body';\n            var parts = [];\n            var cur = el;\n            while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n                var tag = cur.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                var classes = Array.from(cur.classList)\n                    .filter(function (c) { return c.length > 1; })\n                    .slice(0, 2);\n                var part = tag + (classes.length ? '.' + classes.join('.') : '');\n                // add nth-of-type when siblings share the same tag\n                if (cur.parentElement) {\n                    var sibs = Array.from(cur.parentElement.children)\n                        .filter(function (s) { return s.tagName === cur.tagName; });\n                    if (sibs.length > 1) part += ':nth-of-type(' + (sibs.indexOf(cur) + 1) + ')';\n                }\n                parts.unshift(part);\n                cur = cur.parentElement;\n            }\n            return parts.join(' > ');\n        }\n\n        // Highlight box\n        var hlBox = document.createElement('div');\n        hlBox.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'pointer-events:none', 'z-index:9998',\n            'border:2px solid #3b82f6', 'background:rgba(59,130,246,.08)',\n            'border-radius:4px', 'display:none', 'transition:all .08s'\n        ].join(';');\n        document.body.appendChild(hlBox);\n\n        function moveHighlight(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) { hlBox.style.display = 'none'; return; }\n            var r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n            hlBox.style.cssText = hlBox.style.cssText; // keep existing\n            hlBox.style.display = 'block';\n            hlBox.style.left  = (r.left   - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.top   = (r.top    - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.width = (r.width  + 4) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.height= (r.height + 4) + 'px';\n        }\n\n        // Popover\n        var pop = document.createElement('div');\n        pop.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'z-index:9999', 'display:none', 'width:300px',\n            'background:#1e293b', 'border:1px solid #3b82f6', 'border-radius:8px',\n            'padding:12px', 'box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.5)',\n            'font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,\"Segoe UI\",Roboto,sans-serif'\n        ].join(';');\n        pop.innerHTML = [\n            '<div id=\"atn-sel\" style=\"font-size:11px;color:#64748b;margin-bottom:6px;',\n                'font-family:monospace;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\"></div>',\n            '<textarea id=\"atn-txt\" rows=\"3\" placeholder=\"Describe what to change…\" style=\"',\n                'width:100%;box-sizing:border-box;background:#0f1419;border:1px solid #334155;',\n                'color:#e2e8f0;border-radius:6px;padding:8px;font-size:13px;',\n                'resize:none;font-family:inherit;outline:none\"></textarea>',\n            '<div (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n197\t\n198\t                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n199\t                        return child;\n200\t                    }\n201\t                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n202\t                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n203\t                        if (found) return found;\n204\t                    }\n205\t                }\n206\t                return null;\n207\t            }\n208\t            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n209\t            function searchAllElements(element) {\n210\t                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n211\t                    return element;\n212\t                }\n213\t                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n214\t                    for (const child of element._children) {\n215\t                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n216\t                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n217\t                            if (found) return found;\n218\t                        }\n219\t                    }\n220\t                }\n221\t                return null;\n222\t            }\n223\t            return searchAllElements(this);\n224\t            return out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n234\t        }\n235\t        return out;\n236\t    }\n237\t    get outerHTML() {\n238\t        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n246\t        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n247\t        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n248\t            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n249\t            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n250\t                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n251\t                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent)       if (!referenceNode) {\n267\t            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n268\t            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n269\t        }\n270\t        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n271\t        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n272\t        if (refIndex === -1) {\n273\t            // Reference node not found, append to        return newNode;\n279\t    }\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\tglobal.document = {\n283\t    querySelector(selector) {\n284\t        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n285\t        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n286\t        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n287\t        if Bead adc-2vto.\n *\n * The companion server-side suite (tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py)\n * models an SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the server-side drain task that\n * wraps broadcaster.event_generator(). That proves the *server* still delivers\n * to a reconnected stream — but it never drives the browser's own EventSource\n * state machine (onopen → loadTopics, onerror, addEventListener('result_created')\n * → loadTopics, the 'disconnect' → eventSource.close()). This file is the\n * client-side half: it runs the EXACT inline app script the browser runs,\n * under a mock EventSource + mock fetch + minimal DOM shim, and lets a test\n * plan drive open / error / named-event / close / reconnect and observe what\n * the script does in response.\n *\n * What \"reconnect\" means here: a browser EventSource auto-reconnects on\n * transient onerror — the SAME object fires onopen again. That second onopen\n * is the canvas's reconnect path, and it calls loadTopics() (the re-render\n * the AC names). We model reconnect by firing onopen again on the live\n * EventSource — faithful to real browser behavior.\n *\n * The Python driver (tests/test_canvas_eventsource_reconnect.py) feeds a JSON\n * test plan and asserts on the telemetry this prints.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_eventsource_runner.js '<json plan>'\n *   echo '<json plan>' | node canvas_eventsource_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *\n * Plan:\n *   {\n *     \"session_id\": \"...\",          // pre-seeded URL ?session_id=\n *     \"register_surface_id\": \"...\", // what /surfaces/register returns\n *     \"openapi_version\": \"1.2.3\",   // what /openapi.json reports\n *     \"cards\": [ ... ],             // initial GET /topics response\n *     \"steps\": [\n *       {\"action\":\"wait\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"open\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"event\",\"name\":\"result_created\",\"data\":{...}},\n *       {\"action\":\"event\",\"name\":\"disconnect\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"error\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"close\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"reconnect\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"setCards\",\"cards\":[...]}\n *     ]\n *   }\n *\n * Telemetry (stdout, one JSON object):\n *   {\n *     \"initCompleted\": bool,         // connectSSE() ran → an EventSource exists\n *     \"eventSourcesCreated\": int,\n *     \"currentEventSourceUrl\": str,\n *     \"loadTopicsCalls\": int,        // GET /sessions/{id}/topics fetch count\n *     \"statuses\": [{\"status\",\"text\"}...],   // every updateConnectionStatus()\n *     \"closeCalls\": int,\n *     \"containerHTML\": str,\n *     \"containerCardCount\": int,\n *     \"containerCardLabels\": [str...]\n *   }\n */\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\nconst vm = require(\"vm\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim --------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// --- setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n\nconst elementsById = {};\n\nclass El {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._classHistory = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = [];\n        this._style = {};\n        this._disabled = false;\n        this._value = \"\";\n        this._title = \"\";\n        this._textHistory = [];\n        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n        this._display = \"\";\n    }\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n    }\n    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n        };\n    }\n    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n    get style() {\n        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    this._display = value;\n                }\n                target[prop] = value;\n                return true;\n            },\n            get: (target, prop) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    return this._display;\n                }\n                return target[prop];\n            }\n        });\n    }\n    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n    set disabled(v) { this._disabled = !!v; }\n    get value() { return this._value; }\n    set value(v) { this._value = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get title() { return this._title; }\n    set title(v) { this._title = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get scrollHeight() { return 40; }\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n    }\n    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n    }\n    get innerHTML() {\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => {\n                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n                }\n                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n    appendChild(c) {\n        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n        // Fragments have _children array but no _tag property (elements always have _tag)\n        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children) && !c._tag) {\n            // Append all children from the fragment\n            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n                // For elements, add them to _children\n                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                } else if (child) {\n                    // Text nodes\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                }\n            });\n            return c;\n        }\n        // Regular element or text node - append directly\n        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n            this._children.push(c);\n        } else if (c) {\n            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n        }\n        return c;\n    }\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n            const className = selector.slice(1);\n            // Check this element\n            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n                return this;\n            }\n            // Recursively search all descendants\n            function searchDeep(children) {\n                for (const child of children) {\n                    // Skip text nodes in search\n                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n\n                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n                        return child;\n                    }\n                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                        if (found) return found;\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n            function searchAllElements(element) {\n                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n                    return element;\n                }\n                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n                    for (const child of element._children) {\n                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n                            if (found) return found;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            return searchAllElements(this);\n            return searchDeep(this._children);\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n            return this._textContent || \"\";\n        }\n        // Render element with its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n                }\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        } else if (this._innerHTML) {\n            // Use innerHTML string (set via innerHTML setter) - takes precedence over textContent\n            childrenHTML = this._innerHTML;\n        } else if (this._textContent) {\n            // Element has direct textContent (set via textContent setter) but no children/innerHTML\n            // This is the case when _setProgress sets node.textContent = \"X/Y sources in\"\n            childrenHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        }\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n        if (!referenceNode) {\n            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n        if (refIndex === -1) {\n            // Reference node not found, append to end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n        return newNode;\n    }\n}\n\nglobal.document = {\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^\\]]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n            if (attrMatch) {\n                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n                // Search through all elements\n                for (const id in elementsById) {\n                    const el = elementsById[id];\n                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                        return el;\n                    }\n                    // Search recursively through children\n                    function searchDeep(children) {\n                        for (const child of children) {\n                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n                            if (child._dataset && child._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                                return child;\n                            }\n                            if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                                if (found) return found;\n                            }\n                        }\n                        return info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n this.onerror = null;\n        this._listeners = {};\n        this._closed = false;\n        eventSources.push(this);\n    }\n    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n        (this._listeners[name] =     const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n    }\n}\nglobal.EventSource = of Object.keys(global.window)) {\n            if (key !== 'location' && key !== 'history') {  // Keep these as objects\n                global[key] = global.window[key];\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n// Node 21+ exposes a read-only `navigator` getter on the global object, so a\n// plain `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\nObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n\n// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n// the end. First match wins.\n    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n    const m = re.exec(html);\n    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n    return m[1];\n}\n\nconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n};\n\nasync function run() {\n    const plan = readPlan();\n    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n            fetchLog.push(url);\n            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n        };\n    }\n\n    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n    // a browser would provide. SSE reconnection — the browser ``EventSource`` state machine\n(bead adc-2vto).\n\nThe companion server-side suite ``tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py`` models\nan SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the *server-side* drain task wrapping\n``broadcaster.event_generator()`` — it proves the *server* still delivers to a\nreconnected stream, but it never drives the browser's own ``EventSource``.\n\nThis file is the client-side half. It runs the EXACT inline app script the\nbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\nthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\nmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\nplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\nasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":671}}}

_plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass TestInitAndConnect:\n    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n    transitioning the status indicator to \"connecting\" — the baseline a\n    reconnect test starts from.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_init_creates_exactly_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"initCompleted\"] is True\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_eventsource_url_carries_surface_session_and_type(self):\n        \"\"\"The SSE       assert \"surface_id=surf-42\" in url\n        assert \"session_id=sess-42\" in url\n        assert \"surface_type=canvas\" in url\n\n    def test_status_is_connecting_before_open(self):\n \"connecting\", \"text\": \"Connecting...\"}]\n\n\n# === onopen → loadTopics → connected ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestOnOpenLoadsTopics:\n    \"\"\"``onopen`` flips the reconnect (the core AC) ==================================================\n\n\nclass TestReconnect:\n    \"\"\"Native browser ``EventSource`` auto-reconnect: on a transient drop the\n    SAME object fires ``onopen`` again — no new connection is opened. That second\n    ``onopen`` calls ``loadTopics()`` again, so the canvas re-renders with the\n    freshest card set (including anything that arrived during the outage).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_reconnect_does_not_open_a_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A reconnect reuses the existing EventSource — exactly one was ever\n        created, not two.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_reconnect_fires_loadtopics_again(self):\n        \"\"\"The reconnect onopen re-fetches topics — two onopens ⇒ two fetches.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        # Both onopens marked the stream Connected.\n        connected = [s for s in t[\"statuses\"] if s[\"text\"] == \"Connected\"]\n        assert len(connected) == 2\n\n    def test_new_results_appear_after_reconnect(self):\n        \"\"\"A result that arrived during the outage is visible after reconnect:\n        the initial render shows one card; after the server's card set grows and\n        the canvas reconnects, both render.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n      ]},\n                # Reconnect → onopen → loadTopics() re-renders the new set.\n                {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n            ],\n        ))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Delta\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n        assert \"arrived during outage\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n\n# === connection drops: onerror / disconnect / close ==========================\n\n\nclass TestConnectionDrops:\n    \"\"\"The other half of \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting\n    EventSource\": the transient ``onerror`` and the server-initiated\n    ``disconnect`` event, plus an explicit client ``close()``.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_onerror_sets_status_disconnected_without_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"A transient onerror marks the stream disconnected but does NOT close\n        the EventSource — the browser keeps it for auto-reconnect.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"error\"},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_server_disconnect_event_closes_the_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A server ``disconnect`` event marks disconnected AND closes the\n        EventSource (the script's explicit ``eventSource.close()``).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"disconnect\", \"data\": {\"reason\": \"timeout\"}},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n\n    def test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new one.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"close\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n\n# === full lifecycle: drop → reconnect → live event converges ==================\n\n\nclass TestFullLifecycle:\n    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n    error, reconnect, then receive a live result — all on one EventSource.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_drop_reconnect_then_live_event_all_on_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},                       # connect\n                {\"action\": \"error\"},                      # transient drop\n                {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},                  # auto-reconnect\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [         # new result lands\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Epsilon\", topic_id=\"t-e\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\"result_id\": \"r-e\"}},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # Still the single original EventSource throughout.\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n        # open + reconnect + result_created ⇒ three topic fetches.\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 3\n        # Final render reflects the post-reconnect, post-event card set.\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Epsilon\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n        # The status indicator ended back on Connected after the drop.\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"\", \"text\": \"Connected\"}\n\n\n# === pending/ack cards (bead adc-22b1g) ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestPendingAckCards:\n    \"\"\"Submit-time pending placeholder → per-thread pending cards on dispatch-ack.\n    The placeholder appears BEFORE the server round-trip, so a hung server still\n    leaves a card on canvas to age (30s threshold). The dispatch-ack splits it into\n    per-thread cards, each showing per-source progress and elapsed time.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_pending_placeholder_created_at_submit_time(self):\n        \"\"\"A placeholder card is created synchronously at submit, before any\n        server response. It carries the utterance and a creation time.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n        ]))\n        # The inline script's dispatch() is only called from UI handlers (click/key)\n        # which this harness never fires, so we can't directly test the submit path.\n        # But we can verify the placeholder-render functions work by driving them\n        # through the dispatch-ack SSE event which the script does handle.\n        # This test verifies the scaffolding is in place.\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingPlaceholderCount\"] == 0\n\n    def test_dispatch_ack_splits_placeholder_into_thread_cards(self):\n        \"\"\"When a dispatch_ack event arrives with intent_ids, the placeholder splits\n        into per-thread pending cards (one per intent_id). Each inherits the\n        placeholder's creation time so the 30s aged timer is continuous.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-123\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Check the pods\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-a\", \"intent-b\", \"intent-c\"],\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Three thread cards should be created\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 3\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 3\n        # Verify the thread cards have the right structure\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"intent-a\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-b\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-c\" in pending_ids\n        # All threads should be kind=\"thread\"\n        assert all(pc[\"pendingKind\"] == \"thread\" for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"])\n\n    def test_progress_update_updates_thread_card(self):\n        \"\"\"A progress_update SSE event updates a thread card's per-source progress\n        ('3/5 sources in').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-456\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Fetch logs\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-x\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-x\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_multiple_progress_updates_increment_progress(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple progress_update events for the same thread increment the\n        progress counter.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-789\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Poll status\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-y\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Earlier progress values should be gone (replaced, not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_result_created_replaces_pending_card(self):\n        \"\"\"When a result_created event arrives, loadTopics() reloads and the pending\n        card is replaced by the real topic card (no more pending cards).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                    \"utterance_id\": \"utt-999\",\n                    \"utterance\": \"Do the thing\",\n                    \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-z\"],\n                }},\n                # Now the server has the result — swap the card set\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\n                    \"result_id\": \"r-result\",\n                }},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # After result_created, pending cards are gone (replaced by real card)\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 0\n        # The real card is present\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 1\n        assert \"ResultCard\" in t[\"containerCardLabels\"]\n\n\n# === thread_progress events (bead adc-2l7pv) ====================================\n\n\nclass TestThreadProgress:\n    \"\"\"thread_progress SSE events update per-thread pending cards with per-source\n    progress ('3/5 sources in') and elapsed time counters. Uses thread_id targeting\n    (different from progress_update which uses intent_id).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_card_by_thread_id(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event with thread_id finds the matching pending card\n        and updates its progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-1\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Track progress\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-123\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-123\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Verify the specific thread card exists\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"thread-123\" in pending_ids\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_elapsed_time_footer(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event also ensures the elapsed time footer is present\n        on the card (e.g., '0s elapsed').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-2\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Time tracking\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-time\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-time\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 7,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Progress text should be present\n        assert \"2/7 sources in\" in html\n        # Elapsed time footer should be present (format: 'Xs elapsed' or 'Xm Ys elapsed')\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Verify the pending-elapsed element exists\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n    def test_thread_progress_uses_escapeHtml_for_xss_protection(self):\n        \"\"\"Thread progress values are escaped through escapeHtml() to prevent XSS\n        injection via progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-xss\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Test XSS\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-xss\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-xss\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 1,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # The progress text should be rendered as escaped text, not raw HTML\n        # If values were NOT escaped, a script tag would execute\n        # Here we just verify the safe pattern is present\n        assert \"1/1 sources in\" in html\n        # Verify no raw HTML in progress text (textContent is used)\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n\n    def test_multiple_thread_progress_events_update_same_card(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple thread_progress events for the same thread_id update the same\n        card incrementally.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-multi\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Multiple updates\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-multi\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in html\n        # Earlier progress values should be replaced (not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in html\n        assert \"2/4 sources in\" not in html\n        # Should still have exactly one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n\n    def test_thread_progress_with_multiple_threads(self):\n        \"\"\"When multiple thread cards exist, thread_progress events target only\n        the specific thread by thread_id.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-parallel\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Parallel threads\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-a\", \"thread-b\", \"thread-c\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-b\",\n                \"completed\": 5,\n                \"total\": 10,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        #
