That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass TestInitAndConnect:\n    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n    transitioning the status indicator to \"connecting\" — the baseline a\n    reconnect test starts from.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_init_creates_exactly_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"initCompleted\"] is True\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_eventsource_url_carries_surface_session_and_type(self):\n        \"\"\"The SSE URL the canvas opens carries the registered surface_id, the\n        session_id, and surface_type=canvas — the targeting the server filters on.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            session_id=\"sess-42\", surface_id=\"surf-42\",\n            steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}],\n        ))\n        url = t[\"currentEventSourceUrl\"]\n        assert url.startswith(\"/api/v1/sse?\")\n        assert \"surface_id=surf-42\" in url\n        assert \"session_id=sess-42\" in url\n        assert \"surface_type=canvas\" in url\n\n    def test_status_is_connecting_before_open(self):\n        \"\"\"Before the stream's onopen fires, the dot shows \"connecting\".\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"statuses\"] == [{\"status\": \"connecting\", \"text\": \"Connecting...\"}]\n\n\n# === onopen → loadTopics → connected ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestOnOpenLoadsTopics:\n    \"\"\"``onopen`` flips the status to \"Connected\" and re-fetches ``GET /topics``\n    (``loadTopics()``), rendering the current card set — the re-render path a\n    reconnect reuses.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_open_marks_connected_and_fetches_topics_once(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"open\"}]))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 1\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"\", \"text\": \"Connected\"}\n\n    def test_initial_cards_render_on_open(self):\n        \"\"\"The card set present at connect time renders into the container.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", t[\"containerCardLabels\"] == [\"Alpha\"]\n\n    def test_empty_card_set_renders_empty_state(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(cards=[], steps=[{\"action\": \"open\"}]))\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert \"No active topics\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n\n# === named live events re-render ==============================================\n\n\nclass TestLiveEventsReRender:\n    \"\"\"A ``result_created`` or ``topic_updated`` event arriving mid-stream\n    triggers a ``loadTopics()`` re-fetch and re-render — new results appear\n    live, without a reconnect.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"event_name\", [\"result_created\", \"topic_updated\"])\n    def test_named_event_triggers_loadtopics_and_rerender(self, event_name):\n        \"\"\"Start connected with one card, swap the server's card set to two, then\n        deliver the named event: the canvas re-fetches and renders both.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Beta\", topic_id=\"t-b\", topic_type=\"research\", urgency=\"high\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": event_name,\n                 \"data\": {\"topic_id\": \"t-b\", \"result_id\": \"r-1\"}},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # open (1) + the named event (1).\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Beta\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n\n    def test_live_result_event_renders_the_new_card(self):\n        \"\"\"The new card from the event is actually in the rendered container.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"Gamma\", topic_id=\"t-g\", summary=\"build #7 green\", urgency=\"high\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\"result_id\": \"r-g\"}},\n            ],\n        ))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        assert \"Gamma\" in html\n        assert \"build #7 green\" in html\n        assert \"urgency-badge high\" in html\n\n\n# === reconnect (the core AC) ==================================================\n\n\nclass TestReconnect:\n    \"\"\"Native browser ``EventSource`` auto-reconnect: on a transient drop the\n    SAME object fires ``onopen`` again — no new connection is opened. That second\n    ``onopen`` calls ``loadTopics()`` again, so the canvas re-renders with the\n    freshest card set (including anything that arrived during the outage).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_reconnect_does_not_open_a_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A reconnect reuses the existing EventSource — exactly one was ever\n        created, not two.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_reconnect_fires_loadtopics_again(self):\n        \"\"\"The reconnect onopen re-fetches topics — two onopens ⇒ two fetches.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        # Both onopens marked the stream Connected.\n        connected = [s for s in t[\"statuses\"] if s[\"text\"] == \"Connected\"]\n        assert len(connected) == 2\n\n    def test_new_results_appear_after_reconnect(self):\n        \"\"\"A result that arrived during the outage is visible after reconnect:\n        the initial render shows one card; after the server's card set grows and\n        the canvas reconnects, both render.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                # While \"disconnected\" the server gained a new result.\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Delta\", topic_id=\"t-d\", summary=\"arrived during outage\"),\n                ]},\n                # Reconnect → onopen → loadTopics() re-renders the new set.\n                {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},\n            ],\n        ))\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 2\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Delta\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n        assert \"arrived during outage\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n\n# === connection drops: onerror / disconnect / close ==========================\n\n\nclass TestConnectionDrops:\n    \"\"\"The other half of \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting\n    EventSource\": the transient ``onerror`` and the server-initiated\n    ``disconnect`` event, plus an explicit client ``close()``.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_onerror_sets_status_disconnected_without_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"A transient onerror marks the stream disconnected but does NOT close\n        the EventSource — the browser keeps it for auto-reconnect.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"error\"},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_server_disconnect_event_closes_the_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A server ``disconnect`` event marks disconnected AND closes the\n        EventSource (the script's explicit ``eventSource.close()``).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"disconnect\", \"data\": {\"reason\": \"timeout\"}},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n\n    def test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new one.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"close\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n\n# === full lifecycle: drop → reconnect → live event converges ==================\n\n\nclass TestFullLifecycle:\n    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n    error, reconnect, then receive a live result — all on one EventSource.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_drop_reconnect_then_live_event_all_on_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\",           {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},                  # auto-reconnect\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [         # new result lands\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Epsilon\", topic_id=\"t-e\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\"result_id\": \"r-e\"}},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # Still the single original EventSource throughout.\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n        # open + reconnect + result_created ⇒ three topic fetches.\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 3\n        # Final render reflects the post-reconnect, post-event card set.\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Epsilon\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n        # The status indicator ended back on Connected after the drop.\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"\", \"text\": \"Connected\"}\n\n\n# === pending/ack cards (bead adc-22b1g) ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestPendingAckCards:\n    \"\"\"Submit-time pending placeholder → per-thread pending cards on dispatch-ack.\n    The placeholder appears BEFORE the server round-trip, so a hung server still\n    leaves a card on canvas to age (30s threshold). The dispatch-ack splits it into\n    per-thread cards, each showing per-source progress and elapsed time.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_pending_placeholder_created_at_submit_time(self):\n        \"\"\"A placeholder card is created synchronously at submit, before any\n        server response. It carries the utterance and a creation time.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n        ]))\n        # The inline script's dispatch() is only called from UI handlers (click/key)\n        # which this harness never fires, so we can't directly test the submit path.\n        # But we can verify the placeholder-render functions work by driving them\n        # through the dispatch-ack SSE event which the script does handle.\n        # This test verifies the scaffolding is in place.\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingPlaceholderCount\"] == 0\n\n    def test_dispatch_ack_splits_placeholder_into_thread_cards(self):\n        \"\"\"When a dispatch_ack event arrives with intent_ids, the placeholder splits\n        into per-thread pending cards (one per intent_id). Each inherits the\n        placeholder's creation time so the 30s aged timer is continuous.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-123\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Check the pods\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-a\", \"intent-b\", \"intent-c\"],\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Three thread cards should be created\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 3\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 3\n        # Verify the thread cards have the right structure\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"intent-a\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-b\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-c\" in pending_ids\n        # All threads should be kind=\"thread\"\n        assert all(pc[\"pendingKind\"] == \"thread\" for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"])\n\n    def test_progress_update_updates_thread_card(self):\n        \"\"\"A progress_update SSE event updates a thread card's per-source progress\n        ('3/5 sources in').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-456\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Fetch logs\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-x\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-x\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_multiple_progress_updates_increment_progress(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple progress_update events for the same thread increment the\n        progress counter.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-789\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Poll status\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-y\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Earlier progress values should be gone (replaced, not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_result_created_replaces_pending_card(self):\n        \"\"\"When a result_created event arrives, loadTopics() reloads and the pending\n        card is replaced by the real topic card (no more pending cards).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                    \"utterance_id\": \"utt-999\",\n                    \"utterance\": \"Do the thing\",\n                    \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-z\"],\n                }},\n                # Now the server has the result — swap the card set\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\n                    \"result_id\": \"r-result\",\n                }},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # After result_created, pending cards are gone (replaced by real card)\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 0\n        # The real card is present\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 1\n        assert \"ResultCard\" in t[\"containerCardLabels\"]\n\n\n# === thread_progress events (bead adc-2l7pv) ====================================\n\n\nclass TestThreadProgress:\n    \"\"\"thread_progress SSE events update per-thread pending cards with per-source\n    progress ('3/5 sources in') and elapsed time counters. Uses thread_id targeting\n    (different from progress_update which uses intent_id).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_card_by_thread_id(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event with thread_id finds the matching pending card\n        and updates its progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-1\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Track progress\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-123\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-123\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Progress text should be present\n        assert \"2/7 sources in\" in html\n        # Elapsed time footer should be present (format: 'Xs elapsed' or 'Xm Ys elapsed')\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Verify the pending-elapsed element exists\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n    def test_thread_progress_uses_escapeHtml_for_xss_protection(self):\n        \"\"\"Thread progress values are escaped through escapeHtml() to prevent XSS\n        injection via progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-xss\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Test XSS\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-xss\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-xss\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 1,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # The progress text should be rendered as escaped text, not raw HTML\n        # If values were NOT escaped, a script tag would execute\n        # Here we just verify the safe pattern is present\n        assert \"1/1 sources in\" in html\n        # Verify no raw HTML in progress text (textContent is used)\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n\n    def test_multiple_thread_progress_events_update_same_card(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple thread_progress events for the same thread_id update the same\n        card incrementally.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-multi\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Multiple updates\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-multi\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Bead adc-2vto.\n *\n * The companion server-side suite (tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py)\n * models an SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the server-side drain task that\n * wraps broadcaster.event_generator(). That proves the *server* still delivers\n * to a reconnected stream — but it never drives the browser's own EventSource\n * state machine (onopen → loadTopics, onerror, addEventListener('result_created')\n * → loadTopics, the 'disconnect' → eventSource.close()). This file is the\n * client-side half: it runs the EXACT inline app script the browser runs,\n * under a mock EventSource + mock fetch + minimal DOM shim, and lets a test\n * plan drive open / error / named-event / close / reconnect and observe what\n * the script does in response.\n *\n * What \"reconnect\" means here: a browser EventSource auto-reconnects on\n * transient onerror — the SAME object fires onopen again. That second onopen\n * is the canvas's reconnect path, and it calls loadTopics() (the re-render\n * the AC names).  {\"action\":\"close\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"reconnect\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"setCards\",\"cards\":[...]}\n *     ]\n *   }\n *\n * Telemetry (stdout, one JSON object):\n *   {\n * --- minimal DOM shim --------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// --- setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n\nconst elementsById = {};\n\nclass El {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._classHistory = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = [];\n        this._style = {};\n        this._disabled = false;\n        this._value = \"\";\n        this._title = \"\";\n        this._textHistory = [];\n        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n        this._display = \"\";\n    }\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n    }\n    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n        };\n    }\n    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n    get style() {\n        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    this._display = value;\n                }\n                target[prop] = value;\n                return true;\n            },\n            get: (target, prop) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    return this._display;\n                }\n                return target[prop];\n            }\n        });\n    }\n    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n    set disabled(v) { this._disabled = !!v; }\n    get value() { return this._value; }\n    set value(v) { this._value = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get title() { return this._title; }\n    set title(v) { this._title = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get scrollHeight() { return 40; }\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n    }\n    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n    }\n    get innerHTML() {\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => {\n                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n                }\n                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n    appendChild(c) {\n        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n        // Fragments have _children array but no _tag property (elements always have _tag)\n        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children) && !c._tag) {\n            // Append all children from the fragment\n            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n                // For elements, add them to _children\n                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                } else if (child) {\n                    // Text nodes\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                }\n            });\n            return c;\n        }\n        // Regular element or text node - append directly\n        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n            this._children.push(c);\n        } else if (c) {\n            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n        }\n        return c;\n    }\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n            const className = selector.slice(1);\n            // Check this element\n            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n                return this;\n            }\n            // Recursively search all descendants\n            function searchDeep(children) {\n                for (const child of children) {\n                    // Skip text nodes in search\n                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n\n                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n                        return child;\n                    }\n                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                        if (found) return found;\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n            function searchAllElements(element) {\n                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n                    return element;\n                }\n                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n                    for (const child of element._children) {\n                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n                            if (found) return found;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            return searchAllElements(this);\n            return searchDeep(this._children);\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n            return this._textContent || \"\";\n        }\n        // Render element with its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n                }\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        } else if (this._innerHTML) {\n            // Use innerHTML string (set via innerHTML setter) - takes precedence over textContent\n            childrenHTML = this._innerHTML;\n        } else if (this._textContent) {\n            // Element has direct textContent (set via textContent setter) but no children/innerHTML\n            // This is the case when _setProgress sets node.textContent = \"X/Y sources in\"\n            childrenHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        }\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n        if (!referenceNode) {\n            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n        if (refIndex === -1) {\n            // Reference node not found, append to end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n        return newNode;\n    }\n}\n\nglobal.document = {\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^\\]]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n            if (attrMatch) {\n                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n                // Search through all elements\n                for (const id in elementsById) {\n                    const el = elementsById[id];\n                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                        return el;\n                    }\n                    // Search recursively through children\n                    function searchDeep(children) {\n                        for (const child of children) {\n                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n                            if (child._dataset && child._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                                return child;\n                            }\n                            if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                                if (found) return found;\n                            }\n                        }\n                        return null;\n                    }\n                    const found = searchDeep(el._children || []);\n                    if (found) return found;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return null;\n    },\n    getElementById(id) {\n        if (!elementsById[id]) elementsById[id] = new El(\"div\");\n        return elementsById[id];\n    },\n    createElement(tag) { return new El(tag); },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        // Return a minimal text node shim — the el() helper appends these\n        // and our outerHTML render serializes them as escaped text.\n        const node = new El(\"#text\");\n        node._textContent = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        node._innerHTML = escapeText(node._textContent);\n        return node;\n    },\n    createDocumentFragment() {\n        // Return a minimal fragment shim — appendChild tracks children,\n        // and when appended to a real element it spills the children.\n        const frag = {\n            _children: [],\n            childNodes: [],\n            appendChild(child) {\n                this._children.push(child);\n                this.childNodes.push(child);\n                return child;\n            },\n        };\n        return frag;\n    },\n};\n\n// --- mock fetch + EventSource ------------------------------------------------\n\nlet topicsCards = [];\nlet mockSurfaceId = \"surf-mock\";\nconst fetchLog = [];\n\nglobal.fetch = async (url) => {\n    fetchLog.push(url);\n    if (url === \"/openapi.json\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n    }\n    if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/dispatch\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n};\n\nconst eventSources = [];\nlet closeCount = 0;\n\nclass MockEventSource {\n    constructor(url) {\n        this.url = url;\n        this.readyState = 0;       // 0=connecting 1=open 2=closed\n        this.onopen = null;\n        this.onerror = null;\n        this._listeners = {};\n        this._closed = false;\n        eventSources.push(this);\n    }\n    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n        (this._listeners[name] = this._listeners[name] || []).push(cb);\n    }\n    removeEventListener() {}\n    close() { this._closed = true; this.readyState = 2; closeCount++; }\n    // --- driver helpers (used by the harness, not by the app script) ---\n    fireOpen() { this.readyState = 1; if (this.onopen) this.onopen({}); }\n    fireError(e) { if (this.onerror) this.onerror(e || {}); }\n    dispatch(name, dataObj) {\n        const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n    }\n}\nglobal.EventSource = MockEventSource;\n\n// --- globals the inline script touches ---------------------------------------\n\nglobal.window = {\n    location: { search: \"\", pathname: \"/canvas\" },\n    history: { replaceState() {} },\n    // Deliberately NO SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition and NO\n    // navigator.mediaDevices — the inline script's mic block only touches those\n    // inside click handlers, which this harness never fires.\n};\n\n// After canvas.js exports to window, also export those to global scope so\n// bare names like _setProgress work in the inline script (they do in browsers\n// because window is the global scope, but not in Node's vm context).\nfunction syncWindowToGlobal() {\n    if (global.window && typeof global.window === 'object') {\n        for (const key of Object.keys(global.window)) {\n            if (key !== 'location' && key !== 'history') {  // Keep these as objects\n                global[key] = global.window[key];\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n// Node 21+ exposes a read-only `navigator` getter on the global object, so a\n// plain `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\nObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n\n// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n// the end. The REAL inline app script uses console.log for debug noise (\"New\n// result:\", \"Server disconnect:\", \"SSE error:\", …); if that reached stdout it\n// would prefix the telemetry and break the Python driver's JSON.parse. Route all\n// console methods to stderr — the same channel the harness's own error path uses.\nglobal.console = Object.assign({}, console, {\n    log: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    error: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    warn: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    info: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    debug: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n});\nglobal.setInterval = () => 0;   // suppress the 30s heartbeat timer\nglobal.setTimeout = () => 0;\n\n// --- load the REAL render module as globals (as <script src=\"/canvas.js\">) ---\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath);\nglobal.createTopicCard = canvas.createTopicCard;\nglobal.escapeHtml = canvas.escapeHtml;\nglobal.formatStaleness = canvas.formatStaleness;\nglobal.getStalenessLevel = canvas.getStalenessLevel;\nglobal.buildContainerChildren = canvas.buildContainerChildren; First match wins.\n    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n    const m = re.exec(html);\n    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n    return m[1];\n}\n\nconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n};\n\nasync function run() {\n    const plan = readPlan();\n    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n            fetchLog.push(url);\n            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n        };\n    }\n\n    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n    // a browser would provide. The script's top-level init() call schedules the\n    // async registerSurface() → connectSSE() chain.\n    vm.runInThisContext(inline, { filename: \"canvas-inline-script.js\" });\n\n    // Let init() settle: openapi fetch → registerSurface fetch → connectSSE()\n    // creates the (mock) EventSource and wires its handlers.\n    await microtaskDrain(10);\n\n    for (const step of plan.steps || []) {\n        const es = eventSources[eventSources.length - 1];\n        switch (step.action) {\n            case \"wait\":\n                break;\n            case \"open\":\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"error\":\n                if (es) es.fireError();\n                break;\n            case \"event\":\n                if (es) es.dispatch(step.name, step.data);\n                break;\n            case \"close\":\n                if (es) es.close();\n                break;\n            case \"reconnect\":\n                // Native EventSource auto-reconnect: same object fires onopen again.\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"setCards\":\n                topicsCards = Array.isArray(step.cards) ? step.cards : [];\n                break;\n            default:\n                throw new Error(`unknown step action: ${step.action}`);\n        }\n        await microtaskDrain(5);\n    }\n\n    // --- telemetry ---\n    const statusText = elementsById[\"statusText\"];\n    const statusDot = elementsById[\"statusDot\"];\n    const textHist = statusText ? statusText._textHistory : [];\n    const dotHist = statusDot ? statusDot._classHistory : [];\n    const statuses = textHist.map((text, i) => {\n        const dot = dotHist[i] || \"\";\n        return { status: dot.replace(/^status-dot\\s*/, \"\").trim(), text };\n    });\n\n    const container = elementsById[\"topicsContainer\"];\n    const containerHTML = container ? container.innerHTML : \"\";\n    // card labels live in the rendered <topic-header> text; pull them out of the\n    // HTML the real createTopicCard() produced.\n    const containerCardLabels = (containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"topic-header\">([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/div>/g) || [])\n        .map((h) => {\n            // topic-label is a <div> in canvas.js, not a <span>; match the class\n            // and grab visible text up to the next tag so this is tag-agnostic.\n            const m = h.match(/class=\"topic-label\">\\s*([^<]+)/);\n            return m ? m[1].trim() : \"\";\n        });\n\n    // Fallback: if the label class name shifts, best-effort labels from dataset.\n    const containerCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"topic-card/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Count pending cards in the container (placeholders + threads)\n    const pendingPlaceholderCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"placeholder\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingThreadCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"thread\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"[^\"]*pending-card[^\"]*\"/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Extract pending card details for testing\n    const pendingCards = [];\n    const pendingCardMatches = containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"[^\"]*builtin-card pending-card[^\"]*\"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/div>/g) || [];\n    pendingCardMatches.forEach((cardHTML) => {\n        const pendingIdMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-id=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const pendingKindMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const utteranceMatch = cardHTML.match(/<p class=\"pending-utterance\">([^<]*)<\\/p>/);\n        pendingCards.push({\n            pendingId: pendingIdMatch ? pendingIdMatch[1] : null,\n            pendingKind: pendingKindMatch ? pendingKindMatch[1] : null,\n            utterance: utteranceMatch ? utteranceMatch[1].trim() : null,\n        });\n    });\n\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({\n        initCompleted: eventSources.length > 0,\n        eventSourcesCreated: eventSources.length,\n        currentEventSourceUrl: eventSources.length ? eventSources[eventSources.length - 1].url : null,\n        loadTopicsCalls: fetchLog.filter((u) => u.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && u.endsWith(\"/topics\")).length,\n        statuses,\n        closeCalls: closeCount,\n        containerHTML,\n        containerCardCount,\n        containerCardLabels,\n        // Pending card telemetry for bead adc-22b1g\n        pendingPlaceholderCount,\n        pendingThreadCount,\n        pendingCardCount,\n        pendingCards,\n    }));\n}\n\nrun().catch((err) => {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_eventsource_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":277,"oldLines":6,"newStart":277,"newLines":40,"lines":["         this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);","         return newNode;","     }","+    remove() {","+        // Remove this element from its parent's children","+        // In the mock, we need to find the parent and remove this element","+        // Since elementsById tracks top-level elements, we search for a parent","+        for (const id in elementsById) {","+            const parent = elementsById[id];","+            if (parent._children && parent._children.includes(this)) {","+                parent._children = parent._children.filter((c) => c !== this);","+                return;","+            }","+        }","+        // Also search recursively through all children","+        function removeFromParent(children) {","+            for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {","+                const child = children[i];","+                if (child && child._children && child._children.includes(this)) {","+                    child._children = child._children.filter((c) => c !== this);","+                    return true;","+                }","+                if (child && child._children && child._children.length) {","+                    if (removeFromParent(child._children)) {","+                        return true;","+                    }","+                }","+            }","+            return false;","+        }","+        for (const id in elementsById) {","+            const parent = elementsById[id];","+            if (parent._children && removeFromParent(parent._children)) {","+                return;","+            }","+        }","+    }"," }"," "," global.document = {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
